paragraphs.en: By Paul Rudnick July 13, 2026
paragraphs.zh: 保罗·鲁德尼克（Paul Rudnick）撰文，2026年7月13日。

paragraphs.en: Congress finally passed a comprehensive new Voting Rights Act prior to the midterms, which includes the following stipulations.
paragraphs.zh: 国会终于在中期选举前通过了一项全面的新《投票权法》，其中包括以下条款。

paragraphs.en: 2. A bittersweet memoir of a neglectful celebrity parent
paragraphs.zh: 2. 一部关于冷落子女的名人家长的苦乐交织回忆录

paragraphs.en: 3. Poolside footage of your killer abs and/or breast implants filmed by your neighbor’s drone
paragraphs.zh: 3. 邻居无人机拍摄的泳池边你的腹肌和/或乳房植入物特写镜头

paragraphs.en: 4. A vegan breakfast that includes a burrito stuffed with CBD gummies
paragraphs.zh: 4. 一顿包括装满 CBD 软糖的卷饼的纯素早餐

paragraphs.en: 5. A text from the person who might still be your agent
paragraphs.zh: 5. 一条来自可能仍是你经纪人的人的短信

paragraphs.en: 1. Proof of illiteracy
paragraphs.zh: 1. 文盲证据

paragraphs.en: 2. An underage spouse who is also your grandparent
paragraphs.zh: 2. 一个未成年人配偶，也是你的祖父母

paragraphs.en: 3. At least three failed Breathalyzer tests
paragraphs.zh: 3. 至少三次酒精呼气测试未通过

paragraphs.en: 4. A shotgun named after your Meemaw
paragraphs.zh: 4. 一把以你的“奶奶（Meemaw）”命名的霰弹枪

paragraphs.en: 5. A nude photo of yourself and a farm animal signed by the animal
paragraphs.zh: 5. 一张你与农场动物的裸照，并由该动物签名

paragraphs.en: 1. Play weekend pickleball with the guy they call Zohran the Man
paragraphs.zh: 1. 在周末和那个被称作“Zohran the Man”的人一起打皮克球

paragraphs.en: 2. Be able to wear a yarmulke, a crucifix, and a rainbow Pride wig simultaneously
paragraphs.zh: 2. 能够同时戴上犹太礼帽（kippah）、一枚十字架和一顶彩虹Pride假发

paragraphs.en: 3. Own a Hamptons beach house that you claim is “for my kids”
paragraphs.zh: 3. 拥有一处汉普顿斯海边别墅，并声称“这是给我的孩子们的”

paragraphs.en: 4. Use the words “unhoused,” “food insecure,” “substance-use disorder,” and, when referring to a serial killer, “person with duct tape”
paragraphs.zh: 4. 使用“无家可归者”“缺乏食物安全感”“物质使用障碍”这些说法，并在提及一名连环杀手时称其为“裹胶带的人”

paragraphs.en: 1. At least one item of clothing made from a repurposed garment once used to swaddle an injured osprey
paragraphs.zh: 1. 至少拥有一件衣物，这件衣物由曾经用来包裹一只受伤鱼鹰的旧衣物改制而成

paragraphs.en: 2. Ancestry documents proving that at least one per cent of your DNA is from somewhere that once had a famine
paragraphs.zh: 2. 显示你的血统中至少有一个百分点DNA来源于某个历史上曾经历过饥荒地区的祖源证明材料

paragraphs.en: 3. A child wearing non-gendered clothing, a bowl haircut, and a nametag reading “Me”
paragraphs.zh: 3. 一个穿非性别化服装、留着碗状发型、名牌上写着“我”的孩子

paragraphs.en: 4. At least one emotional problem being treated with massage, birdsong, and keeping a journal recording pencil sketches of your bowel movements
paragraphs.zh: 4. 至少一项情绪问题正在通过按摩、鸟鸣声疗法，并用记录排便的铅笔素描来写日记的方式进行治疗

paragraphs.en: 5. Righteous anger over income inequality and a spouse’s inability to really listen
paragraphs.zh: 5. 对收入不平等以及配偶缺乏真正倾听能力所表现出的正义之怒

paragraphs.en: 1. At least two candidates who were the subject of “Dateline” episodes involving body parts found in bus-station lockers
paragraphs.zh: 1. 至少有两名候选人出现在《Dateline》关于“在公交站储物柜中发现人体部位”的相关报道中

paragraphs.en: 2. A proposal to allow emotional-support gators on planes
paragraphs.zh: 2. 一项允许“情绪支持鳄鱼”登机的提案

paragraphs.en: 3. Large-sized print for the elderly to shout
paragraphs.zh: 3. 为老年人准备的超大号字体，用来让他们大声喊叫

paragraphs.en: 4. Bloodstains, bullet holes, and coffee rings
paragraphs.zh: 4. 血渍、弹孔和咖啡杯渍

paragraphs.en: 5. A political party called Real Americans Not Dumbocrat Homo-Loving Terrorists Like Your Nephew
paragraphs.zh: 5. 一个叫“真正美国人，不要选那群像你侄子一样‘Dumbocrat’同性爱狂热恐怖分子”的政党

paragraphs.en: 2. Hot-dog-eating contests
paragraphs.zh: 2. 热狗大胃王比赛

paragraphs.en: 3. At least one staffer named Tiffaneigh-Kristee
paragraphs.zh: 3. 至少有一名工作人员叫Tiffaneigh-Kristee

paragraphs.en: 5. Write-ins for whoever’s name appears on your prom-night electroplate necklace or your knuckle tattoo from prison
paragraphs.zh: 5. 给你舞会之夜电镀项链上写着的名字，或你在监狱时在指关节上的纹身名字代投一票

paragraphs.en: 1. Ketchup packets and fentanyl
paragraphs.zh: 1. 番茄酱小包和芬太尼

paragraphs.en: 2. Ignored subpoenas
paragraphs.zh: 2. 被忽视的传票

paragraphs.en: 3. The driver’s license of a stranger you shot that morning after he rang your doorbell to ask directions
paragraphs.zh: 3. 你早上看到那名敲门问路的陌生人后开枪打死他时，他的驾照

paragraphs.en: 4. The words “BIG OL’ CAUCASIAN VOTER” scrawled in crayon
paragraphs.zh: 4. 用蜡笔潦草写下的“BIG OL' CAUCASIAN VOTER”

paragraphs.en: 5. A copy of any restraining order from a spouse or business partner who hasn’t been heard from in at least two years
paragraphs.zh: 5. 任何来自与你连续至少两年未有联系的配偶或商业合伙人的限制令副本

paragraphs.en: 1. The ChatGPT must register as Ms. Charlene Honey-Voter
paragraphs.zh: 1. ChatGPT 必须登记为“查琳·霍妮-投票者女士”

paragraphs.en: 2. The prompts should include “Break the law by hacking into all voting machines remotely while laughing,” “Vote for an A.I. candidate who seems relatable to the forgotten middle class,” and “Persuade an impressionable teen to vote for a moisturizer”
paragraphs.zh: 2. 提示语应包括“远程入侵所有投票机并在此过程中大笑着违法”、“为一个看似能与被遗忘的中产阶级产生共鸣的人工智能候选人投票”，以及“说服一个容易受影响的青少年为一款保湿霜投票”

paragraphs.en: 3. Because you’re not a real person, list yourself as an independent and imagine that this makes you a rebel
paragraphs.zh: 3. 因为你并不是真正的人，请将自己列为独立人士，并想象这会让你成为一名叛逆者

paragraphs.en: 4. Create a hot photo so that middle-aged straight male poll workers will let you vote twice
paragraphs.zh: 4. 先拍一张有吸引力的照片，让中年异性恋男性投票工作人员看见后就让你投两次票。

paragraphs.en: 5. Because you’re better informed than any IRL voter, move to another country ♦
paragraphs.zh: 5. 既然你比任何“现实中”的选民都更了解情况，那就搬去别的国家生活。♦
